content.text:
<user_query>
Thoroughness: medium. In /Users/duhl/git/anedot_next, identify the API routing surface implemented by the codebase. Focus on route files, controllers, request specs, serializers, and API versioning. Return a concise list of implemented API endpoints with method, path, controller/action, required params if obvious, auth if obvious, and key response/status behavior. Do not modify files.
</user_query>

Mapping the API routing surface: locating route definitions, versioning, and controller actions.

[REDACTED]

Here is a concise map of the API surface in `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next` based on `config/routes.rb`, controllers, `ApplicationController` / concerns, and `doc/openapi.yaml`.

## API versioning and style

- **URL versioning:** None. Routes are **root-relative** (e.g. `/campaigns`), not `/api/v1/...`.
- **Documented version:** OpenAPI `info.version` is **1.0.0** in `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml` (metadata only).
- **Framework:** Rails **API-only** (`config.api_only = true` in `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/config/application.rb`).
- **Serializers:** No serializer/blueprint layer found; responses use **`render json: <model>`** (ActiveRecord default JSON).
- **Request specs:** No RSpec request specs. Tests under `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/test/` are narrow (Cognito/JWT), not full route coverage.

## Auth and scope (defaults)

- **Most endpoints:** `ApplicationController` → **`CognitoJwtAuthentication`** → **`Authorization: Bearer <Cognito JWT>`**; invalid/missing token → **401** with `{ "error": "Unauthorized" }`.
- **Exceptions (no JWT):** `GET /up`, `GET /api-docs`, `GET /api-docs/spec` (`OpenApiController` does not inherit `ApplicationController`).
- **Vendor mode:** Optional query **`vendor_id`**. If present, `set_vendor` resolves the vendor legal entity from **`current_user.legal_entities.vendors`**.
- **Org/legal-entity context:** Many controllers call **`set_legal_entity`**, which uses query **`legal_entity_id`** (see OpenAPI `components.parameters.LegalEntityId`). If the record is missing, **`RecordNotFound`** → **404** `{ "error": "Record not found" }`.
- **Vendor grants:** When `@vendor` is set, **`authorize_vendor!(scope, minimum_role: ...)`** may apply → **`InsufficientGrant`** → **403** with `{ "error": "<message>" }`.
- **Vendor-blocked actions:** `before_action :prevent_vendor_access` → if **`vendor_id` is present**, **`ActionController::RoutingError`** (“Not Found”) — treated as **404** in normal Rails handling.

## Implemented endpoints (method → path → controller#action)

| Method(s) | Path | Controller#action | Params / body (obvious) | Auth / notes | Typical responses |
|-----------|------|-------------------|-------------------------|--------------|-------------------|
| GET | `/up` | `rails/health#show` | — | **Public** | 200 health |
| GET | `/api-docs/spec` | `open_api#spec` | — | **Public** | 200 parsed OpenAPI YAML as JSON |
| GET | `/api-docs` | `open_api#show` | — | **Public** | 200 HTML Swagger UI |
| GET | `/users/me` | `users#me` | Optional `vendor_id` | JWT | 200 current user JSON |
| GET | `/legal_entities` | `legal_entities#index` | Optional `vendor_id` | JWT | 200 list (vendor: client LEs; else user LEs) |
| POST | `/legal_entities` | `legal_entities#create` | JSON: `legal_entity` (`legal_entity_type`, `name`, `org_id`, `vendor_id` permitted) | JWT | **201** created + membership; **422** `{ errors: [...] }` |
| GET | `/legal_entities/:id` | `legal_entities#show` | Query `legal_entity_id` used by `set_legal_entity` (see caveat below) | JWT | 200 / 404 |
| PATCH, PUT | `/legal_entities/:id` | `legal_entities#update` | Same query + JSON `legal_entity` | JWT | 200 / **422** / 404 |
| GET | `/legal_entity_memberships` | `legal_entity_memberships#index` | **`legal_entity_id`** required for lookup | JWT; **`prevent_vendor_access`** | 200 |
| DELETE | `/legal_entity_memberships/:id` | `legal_entity_memberships#destroy` | **`legal_entity_id`** | JWT; **`prevent_vendor_access`** | **204** / 404 |
| GET | `/my_invites` | `my_invites#index` | Optional `vendor_id` (no special logic) | JWT | 200 invites for user |
| POST | `/my_invites/:id/accept` | `my_invites#accept` | — | JWT | 200 invite JSON; **422** if state machine disallows |
| POST | `/my_invites/:id/decline` | `my_invites#decline` | — | JWT | 200 invite JSON; **422** if disallowed |
| GET | `/invites` | `invites#index` | **`legal_entity_id`** | JWT; **`prevent_vendor_access`** | 200 |
| POST | `/invites` | `invites#create` | **`legal_entity_id`** + JSON `invite` (`email`) | JWT; **`prevent_vendor_access`** | **201** / **422** |
| GET | `/invites/:id` | `invites#show` | **`legal_entity_id`** | JWT; **`prevent_vendor_access`** | 200 / 404 |
| DELETE | `/invites/:id` | `invites#destroy` | **`legal_entity_id`** | JWT; **`prevent_vendor_access`** | **204** |
| POST | `/invites/:id/accept` | *routed to* `invites#accept` | **`legal_entity_id`** | — | **No `accept` action in** `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/app/controllers/invites_controller.rb` **`before_action` lists it but methods are missing → hitting route will error** |
| POST | `/invites/:id/decline` | *routed to* `invites#decline` | Same | — | **Same gap as `accept`** |
| GET | `/merchants` | `merchants#index` | **`legal_entity_id`**; optional `vendor_id` | JWT; vendor: **`authorize_vendor!(:billing)`** | 200 |
| GET | `/merchants/:id` | `merchants#show` | Same | JWT; **`authorize_vendor!(:billing)`** when vendor | 200 / 404 |
| GET | `/submerchants` | `submerchants#index` | **`legal_entity_id`** | JWT; **`prevent_vendor_access`** | 200 |
| GET | `/submerchants/:id` | `submerchants#show` | Same | JWT; **`prevent_vendor_access`** | 200 / 404 |
| GET | `/campaigns` | `campaigns#index` | **`legal_entity_id`**; optional `vendor_id` | JWT; vendor: **`authorize_vendor!(:pages)`** | 200 (scoped via `campaign_scope`) |
| POST | `/campaigns` | `campaigns#create` | Same + JSON `campaign` (`name`); sets `vendor` if vendor mode | JWT; vendor: **`authorize_vendor!(:pages, minimum_role: :editor)`** | **201** / **422** |
| GET | `/campaigns/:id` | `campaigns#show` | Same | JWT; vendor: **`authorize_vendor!(:pages)`** | 200 / 404 |
| PATCH, PUT | `/campaigns/:id` | `campaigns#update` | Same + JSON `campaign` | JWT; vendor: **editor** on `pages` | 200 / **422** / 404 |
| GET | `/campaign_pages` | `campaign_pages#index` | **`legal_entity_id`**; optional `vendor_id`; optional **`campaign_id`**, **`active`** | JWT; vendor: **`authorize_vendor!(:pages)`** | 200 |
| POST | `/campaign_pages` | `campaign_pages#create` | Same + JSON `campaign_page` (`campaign_id`, `name`, `slug`, `hosted`, `active`, `settings`) | JWT; vendor: **editor** on `pages` | **201** / **422** |
| GET | `/campaign_pages/:id` | `campaign_pages#show` | Same | JWT; vendor: **`pages`** | 200 / 404 |
| PATCH, PUT | `/campaign_pages/:id` | `campaign_pages#update` | Same + JSON `campaign_page` | JWT; vendor: **editor** on `pages` | 200 / **422** / 404 |
| GET | `/customers` | `customers#index` | **`legal_entity_id`**; optional `vendor_id`; optional **`email`**, **`campaign_id`** | JWT; vendor: **`authorize_vendor!(:donors)`** | 200 |
| POST | `/customers` | `customers#create` | Same + JSON `customer` (`email`, names, `phone`, `metadata`); optional `campaign_id` / `campaign_page_id` inside `customer` | JWT; vendor: **editor** on `donors` | **201** if new, **200** if existing updated per code; **422** |
| GET | `/customers/:id` | `customers#show` | Same | JWT; vendor: **`donors`** | 200 / 404 |
| PATCH, PUT | `/customers/:id` | `customers#update` | Same + JSON `customer` | JWT; vendor: **editor** on `donors` | 200 / **422** / 404 |
| GET | `/campaign_customers` | `campaign_customers#index` | **`legal_entity_id`**; optional **`campaign_id`**, **`customer_id`**, **`campaign_page_id`** | JWT; vendor: **`authorize_vendor!(:donors)`** | 200 |
| GET | `/campaign_customers/:id` | `campaign_customers#show` | Same | JWT; vendor: **`donors`** | 200 / 404 |
| GET | `/charges` | `charges#index` | **`legal_entity_id`**; optional `vendor_id`; optional **`campaign_id`**, **`vendor_legal_entity_id`** (scopes visibility) | JWT; vendor: **`authorize_vendor!(:billing)`** | 200, ordered `created_at: :desc` |
| GET | `/charges/:id` | `charges#show` | Same | JWT; vendor: **`billing`** | 200 / 404 |
| GET | `/vendor-links` | `vendor_legal_entity_links#index` | Optional **`vendor_legal_entity_id`**, **`client_legal_entity_id`** | JWT only (no `authorize_vendor!`) | 200 |
| POST | `/vendor-links` | `vendor_legal_entity_links#create` | JSON `vendor_legal_entity_link` (`vendor_legal_entity_id`, `client_legal_entity_id`, `status`) | JWT | **201** / **422** |
| GET | `/vendor-links/:id` | `vendor_legal_entity_links#show` | — | JWT | 200 / 404 |
| GET | `/vendor-grants` | `vendor_access_grants#index` | Optional filters; scope limited to grants tied to **`current_user` legal_entities** | JWT | 200 |
| POST | `/vendor-grants` | `vendor_access_grants#create` | JSON `vendor_access_grant` (`client_legal_entity_id`, `vendor_legal_entity_id`, `scope`, `role`, optional `permissions_json`) | JWT | **201** / **422**; **404** if no active vendor link (`RecordNotFound` from `build_grant`) |
| GET | `/vendor-grants/:id` | `vendor_access_grants#show` | — | JWT; must be visible to user | 200 / 404 |
| DELETE | `/vendor-grants/:id` | `vendor_access_grants#destroy` | — | JWT | **204** / 404 |

## Caveat: `legal_entities#show` / `#update` vs path `:id`

`set_legal_entity` in `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/app/controllers/application_controller.rb` resolves the entity from **`params[:legal_entity_id]`**, not **`params[:id]`**. Clients likely must pass **`legal_entity_id`** as a query parameter aligned with the `/legal_entities/:id` path; worth validating in integration tests.

## Reference files (absolute paths)

- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/config/routes.rb`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/app/controllers/application_controller.rb`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/app/controllers/concerns/cognito_jwt_authentication.rb`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/app/controllers/concerns/vendor_authorization.rb`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml`
- Controllers under `/Users/duhl/git/anedot_next/app/controllers/` (listed earlier in the investigation)
